entries.name: K9-Bench: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs on Canine-Centric Videos

frontmatter.title: K9-Bench: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs on Canine-Centric Videos
frontmatter.tldr: Benchmarks long-horizon multimodal reasoning over domestic dog videos and exposes limits in subtle temporal and compositional understanding.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Provides approximately 5,000 question-answer pairs over 907 videos across five task categories, with a VLM/LLM-assisted data-construction pipeline and bias-mitigation steps.

frontmatter.title: AGC-Bench: Measuring Artificial General Creativity
frontmatter.tldr: Builds a broad creativity benchmark and judge-calibration pipeline for comparing language-model creativity across domains.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Releases 78 creativity datasets with an agentic harness and applies Judge Response Theory to calibrate evaluator severity before producing an open AGC-Judge model.

entries.name: Managing Procedural Memory in LLM Agents: Control, Adaptation, and Evaluation

frontmatter.title: Managing Procedural Memory in LLM Agents: Control, Adaptation, and Evaluation
frontmatter.tldr: Introduces AFTER, a benchmark for measuring how procedural skills transfer across tasks, roles, and model backbones.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Defines 382 enterprise-style tasks across six roles and 22 procedural skills with local-improvement, cross-task, cross-role, and cross-model evaluation settings.

entries.name: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks

frontmatter.title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
frontmatter.tldr: Introduces a capability-driven benchmark for proactive agents using 400 bilingual real-world tasks, live Docker environments, fine-grained checkpoints, and closed-loop feedback.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Separates skill usage, exploration, long-context reasoning, multimodal understanding, and cross-platform coordination while evaluating agents in live containers and across multiple frameworks.

entries.name: AgenticSTS: A Bounded-Memory Testbed for Long-Horizon LLM Agents

frontmatter.title: AgenticSTS: A Bounded-Memory Testbed for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
frontmatter.tldr: Introduces a bounded-memory testbed in which long-horizon agent decisions use typed retrieval instead of an ever-growing raw transcript.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Defines a memory contract with fresh bounded prompts, isolatable memory and skill layers, and a reproducible game-based testbed for studying long-horizon agent decisions.

entries.name: Regression Accumulation in Multi-Turn LLM Programming Conversations

frontmatter.title: Regression Accumulation in Multi-Turn LLM Programming Conversations
frontmatter.tldr: Shows that iterative coding conversations can silently break earlier requirements, and evaluates a verification gate that retests prior behavior.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Builds 542 HumanEval+ and MBPP+ tasks into eight-turn requirement-evolution chains and evaluates preservation of earlier tests across 26,016 model-turn instances.

frontmatter.title: Meta-Benchmarks for Financial-Services LLM Evaluation
frontmatter.tldr: Organizes public benchmarks by financial work activities and domains, weighting them by discrimination, coverage, and recency for more decision-relevant model comparisons.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Maps 452 reported benchmarks to 41 O*NET work activities and 38 BIAN banking domains, then uses weighted pairwise Elo aggregation instead of raw score normalization.

entries.name: Evaluating Chunking Strategies for Retrieval-Augmented Generation on Academic Texts

frontmatter.title: Evaluating Chunking Strategies for Retrieval-Augmented Generation on Academic Texts
frontmatter.tldr: Finds that semantic cluster chunking did not beat simpler chunking in the tested academic-text RAG setup and questions the reliability of one faithfulness metric.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Compares cluster-based semantic, fixed-size, and recursive chunking on long structured theses using RAGAs-style evaluation, including fixed and document-specific questions.

entries.name: Benchmarking Code Improvement with Progressive, Adaptive, and Interactive Feedback

frontmatter.title: Benchmarking Code Improvement with Progressive, Adaptive, and Interactive Feedback
frontmatter.tldr: Proposes a code-improvement benchmark that measures repair trajectories, hint use, regressions, and partial progress instead of only final pass or fail.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Defines progressive hinting with failure-region and hint-depth controls so evaluations can measure targeted repair, generalization, preservation of correct behavior, and assistance required.

entries.name: Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution

frontmatter.title: Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution
frontmatter.tldr: Calibrates LLM judges for citation quality in deep research and shows that cheaper judges can compete with frontier models while differing in directional bias and pass-rate drift.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Evaluates eight judges across 1,248 rubric decisions, separates source relevance from factual support, and shows that scalar F1 can hide false-positive/false-negative bias that a reinforcement-learning loop would reinforce.

entries.name: ADR: An Agentic Detection System for Enterprise Agentic AI Security

frontmatter.title: ADR: An Agentic Detection System for Enterprise Agentic AI Security
frontmatter.tldr: Presents a production-oriented detection and response system for MCP-connected agents with telemetry, red-team exploration, and two-tier online detection.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Combines an agent telemetry sensor, an exploration/red-teaming component, and a scalable detector; it also introduces ADR-Bench and reports author-described deployment and benchmark results that require independent verification.

entries.name: Dual-Confidence Contrastive Decoding for Retrieval-Augmented Generation

frontmatter.title: Dual-Confidence Contrastive Decoding for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
frontmatter.tldr: Introduces confidence-gated contrastive decoding for conflicts among multiple retrieved documents in RAG.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Combines document-level sufficiency confidence with token-level predictive confidence to select positive and negative document-conditioned streams for training-free decoding.

entries.name: From Tool Connection to Execution Control: Benchmarking Security Invariants in MCP-Style Agent Runtimes

frontmatter.title: From Tool Connection to Execution Control: Benchmarking Security Invariants in MCP-Style Agent Runtimes
frontmatter.tldr: Defines explicit execution-layer security invariants for MCP-style agent runtimes and evaluates a capability-oriented reference design against connection-layer baselines.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Formalizes metadata non-authority, grant-backed approval, principal binding, scoped capabilities, data-flow authorization, deny-path audit, and protocol state; the paper reports a ten-case modeled evaluation rather than universal attack coverage.

entries.name: AgentAtlas: Beyond Outcome Leaderboards for LLM Agents

frontmatter.title: AgentAtlas: Beyond Outcome Leaderboards for LLM Agents
frontmatter.tldr: Proposes a diagnostic vocabulary for separating agent outcome success from control decisions, trajectory failures, and benchmark coverage instead of relying on one final success score.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Defines six control-decision states, a trajectory-failure vocabulary, and a 0/1/2 coverage audit over fifteen agent benchmarks; the illustrative 1,342-item study is explicitly not a public benchmark or definitive model leaderboard.

entries.name: Retrieval as Reasoning: Self-Evolving Agent-Native Retrieval via LLM-Wiki

frontmatter.title: Retrieval as Reasoning: Self-Evolving Agent-Native Retrieval via LLM-Wiki
frontmatter.tldr: Recasts retrieval for agents as search, reading, link traversal, and evidence sufficiency decisions over a compiled, self-evolving wiki rather than flat embedding lookup.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Introduces LLM-Wiki with structured wiki pages, bidirectional links, tool-calling search/read/follow operations, and an Error Book for structural and semantic correction; the paper reports gains on multi-hop retrieval benchmarks.

entries.name: Clinician-Level Agreement Without Clinical Caution: LLM Evaluator Limits in Medical AI Benchmarking

frontmatter.title: Clinician-Level Agreement Without Clinical Caution: LLM Evaluator Limits in Medical AI Benchmarking
frontmatter.tldr: Introduces a German open-response clinical benchmark and shows that evaluator agreement can coexist with poor clinical abstention behavior.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Introduces MedQADE with 3,800 items annotated by ten physicians and nine LLM evaluators, then compares agreement, calibration, abstention, and lineage bias.

entries.name: Validity of LLMs as Data Annotators: AMALIA on Authority

frontmatter.title: Validity of LLMs as Data Annotators: AMALIA on Authority
frontmatter.tldr: Tests whether a national language model’s agreement with human annotation reflects valid construct reasoning rather than surface-correlated shortcuts.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Introduces a recovery-gap diagnostic that decomposes a holistic annotation prompt into theory-grounded clauses and tests whether agreement survives the decomposition.

entries.name: AGI Maze as a Benchmark Framework for World-Modeling Agents

frontmatter.title: AGI Maze as a Benchmark Framework for World-Modeling Agents
frontmatter.tldr: Presents lightweight partially observable maze environments designed to test whether agents build and use persistent world-state representations.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Introduces a clean API and multiple difficulty regimes for grid-based world-modeling tasks that separate stateful reasoning from static pattern completion.

entries.name: GateMem: Benchmarking Memory Governance in Multi-Principal Shared-Memory Agents

frontmatter.title: GateMem: Benchmarking Memory Governance in Multi-Principal Shared-Memory Agents
frontmatter.tldr: Benchmarks utility, authorization, and active forgetting when multiple principals share an agent memory pool.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Defines multi-principal episodes with contextual access boundaries, state updates, deletion requests, structured judging, and leak-target annotations.

entries.name: ContextNest: Verifiable Context Governance for Autonomous AI Agent

frontmatter.title: ContextNest: Verifiable Context Governance for Autonomous AI Agent
frontmatter.tldr: Proposes a governed knowledge-vault layer that verifies provenance, version identity, integrity, and point-in-time eligibility before retrieval.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Defines context governance using typed documents and metadata, deterministic selectors, hash-chained history, graph checkpoints, source nodes, and audit traces for agent context consumption.

entries.name: Agent Planning Benchmark: A Diagnostic Framework for Planning Capabilities in LLM Agents

frontmatter.title: Agent Planning Benchmark: A Diagnostic Framework for Planning Capabilities in LLM Agents
frontmatter.tldr: Introduces a planning-specific agent benchmark with multimodal cases, tool noise, broken tools, unsolvable tasks, feedback-conditioned planning, and inference-time refinement.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Provides 4,209 cases across 22 domains and five planning settings, then evaluates whether planning diagnostics improve downstream execution on ToolSandbox and tau2-bench tasks.

entries.name: A Hierarchical Memory Architecture Overcomes Context Limits in Long-Horizon Multi-Agent Computational Modeling

frontmatter.title: A Hierarchical Memory Architecture Overcomes Context Limits in Long-Horizon Multi-Agent Computational Modeling
frontmatter.tldr: Describes a five-worker multi-agent system with three-layer hierarchical memory for long-running pharmacokinetic modeling.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Introduces bounded hierarchical project memory with category caps and eviction, plus domain-expert oversight and physics-based checklists for computational modeling.

entries.name: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents

frontmatter.title: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents
frontmatter.tldr: Uses a separate memory agent to decide when to inject a structured, memory-grounded reminder into a long-horizon action agent.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Frames memory as selective intervention: a memory module updates structured state and either injects a reminder or stays silent, leaving the action agent otherwise unchanged.

entries.name: AgentGym2: Benchmarking Large Language Model Agents in De-Idealized Real-World Environments

frontmatter.title: AgentGym2: Benchmarking Large Language Model Agents in De-Idealized Real-World Environments
frontmatter.tldr: Proposes a de-idealized agent benchmark where agents must explore tools, handle noisy or underspecified inputs, compose actions, and complete end-to-end real-world procedures.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Moves agent evaluation beyond clean pre-packaged tools by measuring exploration, tool composition, robustness to noise, and execution under realistic task requirements.

entries.name: MemSyco-Bench: Benchmarking Sycophancy in Agent Memory

frontmatter.title: MemSyco-Bench: Benchmarking Sycophancy in Agent Memory
frontmatter.tldr: Benchmarks when retrieved agent memories should influence decisions, and when agents should reject or scope those memories.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Introduces five tests for memory-induced sycophancy: rejecting memory as factual evidence, respecting scope, resolving memory/evidence conflicts, tracking updates, and using valid memory for personalization.

entries.name: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments

frontmatter.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
frontmatter.tldr: Benchmarks agents as terminal, software, and social environments change over progressive updates, pairing the suite with patch-based memory.
frontmatter.key_contribution: Introduces EvoArena and EvoMem, which records memory evolution as structured update histories instead of treating memory as a static store.

frontmatter.name: Giskard OSS
frontmatter.description: Open-source evaluation and red-team testing library for LLM agents, RAG systems, and model behavior
frontmatter.pricing_detail: Open-source evaluation library; hosted or enterprise options, if needed, are separate from the OSS core
frontmatter.verdict_rationale: Promising open evaluation and red-team surface, but current coverage and workflow fit need hands-on verification
best_when: You need one open framework for LLM/RAG/agent testing and security-oriented evaluation
You want evaluation findings to become regression tests rather than isolated manual reports
avoid_when: You need a single specialized benchmark with a fixed leaderboard protocol
You require production effectiveness claims without calibrating evaluators and test suites on your domain
frontmatter.enrichment_notes: Repository metadata, Apache-2.0 license, and active July 2026 maintenance were reviewed on 2026-07-11; evaluator coverage and production fit remain draft.

frontmatter.name: Headroom
frontmatter.description: Context-compression library, proxy, and MCP server for reducing tool, log, file, and RAG token load
frontmatter.pricing_detail: Open-source software; self-hosted compute and model costs are the operator's responsibility
frontmatter.verdict_rationale: Promising context-efficiency layer with strong testing signals, but live production behavior needs independent verification
best_when: You need to reduce repeated tool, log, file, or RAG context before it reaches the model
You can operate a fail-open proxy/MCP layer and measure answer retention on your own workloads
avoid_when: You need lossless raw context for every request
You cannot tolerate compression latency, fallback behavior, or per-process cache limitations
frontmatter.enrichment_notes: Official repository, Apache-2.0 license, current version alignment, focused tests, and fail-open safeguards reviewed on 2026-07-11; headline token savings are not independently validated here.

frontmatter.name: llmfit
frontmatter.description: Local model-selection CLI that matches models and providers to available hardware and runtime constraints
frontmatter.pricing_detail: Open-source local CLI; model downloads and inference hardware are operator-managed
frontmatter.verdict_rationale: Useful model-to-hardware routing concept, but model metadata freshness and recommendation quality need independent checks
best_when: You need a quick local answer to which model/provider can fit a given machine
You want hardware-aware model discovery before manually downloading and benchmarking candidates
avoid_when: You need authoritative production capacity planning or SLA guarantees
You require recommendations grounded in your own latency, quality, and cost benchmarks
frontmatter.enrichment_notes: Official repository, MIT license, Rust implementation, and active July 2026 maintenance were reviewed on 2026-07-11; model catalog freshness and fit recommendations remain draft.

frontmatter.name: RTK
frontmatter.description: Rust CLI proxy that reduces token-heavy developer command output before it reaches coding agents
frontmatter.pricing_detail: Open-source single-binary CLI; operator pays only local installation and model usage costs
frontmatter.verdict_rationale: Interesting low-dependency token-efficiency proxy, but savings and compatibility need workload-specific verification
best_when: Your coding agents repeatedly ingest verbose shell, git, test, or build output
You want a local Rust binary with no Python runtime dependency
avoid_when: Your workflows depend on exact unmodified command output
You need a provider-neutral server-side observability or budget-control layer rather than a local CLI proxy
frontmatter.enrichment_notes: Official repository, Apache-2.0 license, Rust implementation, and current development were reviewed on 2026-07-11; the 60–90% savings claim remains unverified.

frontmatter.name: ToolHive
frontmatter.description: Enterprise-oriented platform for running, isolating, and managing Model Context Protocol servers
frontmatter.pricing_detail: Open-source self-hosted platform; operator-managed Kubernetes and infrastructure costs apply
frontmatter.verdict_rationale: Relevant MCP isolation and operations layer, with deployment and policy details still requiring hands-on review
best_when: You need a managed operational boundary around multiple MCP servers
You need deployment, isolation, and lifecycle controls rather than a bare MCP process
avoid_when: You only run one trusted local MCP server
You need a lightweight single-binary developer setup with no cluster or platform overhead
frontmatter.enrichment_notes: Repository metadata, Apache-2.0 license, Go implementation, and active July 2026 maintenance were reviewed on 2026-07-11; isolation and deployment guarantees remain draft.
